visual_observations: Island: small island clearly completely surrounded by calm ocean water — CONFIRMED
Ruin structures: dense cluster of large multi-story abandoned concrete buildings covering the island — CONFIRMED
Island shape: distinctive elongated 'battleship' profile consistent with Hashima/Gunkanjima — CONFIRMED
Dusk sky: warm amber and deep orange gradient sky filling upper two-thirds of frame — CONFIRMED
Horizon glow: sun near or just below horizon, warm light emanating from horizon behind/beside the island — CONFIRMED
Sea water: calm water surrounding island on all sides, soft golden reflections from dusk sky — CONFIRMED
Three-band composition: sky (top ~60%) / island ruins (center) / reflective sea (bottom) — CONFIRMED
No people: confirmed — zero human forms anywhere
No vessels: confirmed — no boats, ships, or ferries in frame

extreme_wide_island_view.detail: Island appears at middle distance surrounded by sea. Not a close-up. Extreme wide perspective confirmed.

ocean_clearly_visible.detail: Calm ocean visible in foreground, flanking both sides of the island, and implied behind. Water fills approximately the lower 30% of the frame.

island_surrounded_by_sea.detail: Island is a distinct landmass in the middle of the ocean. The corrected prompt anchor 'small island completely surrounded by sea' was honoured.

warm_restrained_dusk_palette.detail: Warm amber and orange-gold tones throughout. The palette is vivid but cinematic — within documentary range for a dusk establishing shot. Minor note: slightly more saturated than 'warm desaturated' may imply. Post-processing desaturation recommended to align with dark_documentary grade (see post_process_notes).

calm_reflective_water.detail: Sea surface is calm with soft golden reflections from the dusk sky. No waves or storm conditions.

no_vessels.detail: No boats, ships, or tourist ferries in frame.

not_building_close_up.detail: Attempt 1 failure (building facade close-up) completely corrected. This image is a distant wide view of the entire island.

not_visually_identical_to_IMG007.detail: IMG007 (S008) is a close-up low-angle building facade in cool grey. This is an extreme wide warm-dusk sea view. Completely different in scale, color, composition, and context. No visual collision.

visually_distinct_from_IMG001.detail: IMG001 (S001 HOOK) is cold dark blue-black pre-dawn, island as dark silhouette. This is warm orange-amber dusk with illuminated ruin detail. Opposite color temperature. These two images form the visual bookends of the documentary — cold mystery at opening, warm resolution before the closing silence. Contrast is intentional and effective.

visually_distinct_from_IMG020.detail: IMG020 (S024) is a 1974 Japan Government aerial photograph, desaturated/monochrome, top-down view. This is a warm-color sea-level contemporary wide. No visual overlap.

visually_distinct_from_IMG019.detail: IMG019 (S023 OUTRO) shows a ferry bow in the foreground with the island at middle distance, cool overcast grey. This image has no vessel foreground and a warm dusk palette. Clearly distinct.

suitable_before_40s_ma_silence.detail: The warm island at dusk surrounded by calm sea is exactly the kind of image that carries a 40-second contemplative silence. Serene, majestic, vast. The image holds visual interest across 40 seconds of very_slow_pan_right through the warm sky and reflective sea.

respectful_documentary_tone.detail: Beautiful cinematic landscape. No sensationalism, no horror, no tragedy imagery. The tone is reverent and contemplative — appropriate for the UNESCO inscription narration and the approaching Ma silence.

slow_pan_compatible.detail: Wide horizontal composition with open sea on both sides of the island and warm sky above. The very_slow_pan_right at 40s will sweep from the island across the warm reflective sea to the right — effective cinematic motion for this scene.

minor_notes.description: Sky palette is slightly more vivid/saturated than 'warm desaturated amber' may imply. The orange is rich and cinematic but not quite documentary-neutral.
minor_notes.action: POST-PROCESS: apply slight desaturation (reduce saturation by ~15-20%) to bring warm tones in line with the overall dark_documentary grade. Preserve the warm amber relative to the cold ACT_I and ACT_II scenes — do not flatten to grey. The warm vs cold contrast between S020 and earlier scenes is a deliberate narrative tool.

post_process_notes: Film grain in prompt. Apply very_slow_pan_right at 40-second duration (S020 timecode 9:00–9:40). Slight desaturation in post to align with dark_documentary grade — preserve warmth relative to earlier cold scenes but reduce peak saturation. Narration L027 (UNESCO 2015 inscription) plays over this image before 40-second Ma silence begins.
upgrade_flag_status: STILL VALID — not a production blocker
upgrade_flag_note: PIXTA paid dusk photography (軍艦島 夕暮れ, ~¥5K-¥15K RF) would produce real photographic depth of field and authentic Hashima atmosphere at this position. The AI image is production-ready and sufficient to complete the pipeline. Upgrade is recommended as a pre-publish enhancement, not a gate.
image_asset_phase_note: All 19 non-suppressed images are now approved or registered. IMG005 is SUPPRESSED. Image asset phase is COMPLETE.
